subject: Chemistry
title: The Great Work: Stages of Transmutation
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Great Work: Stages of Transmutation.

hook.text: Alchemists described transmutation as the 'Magnum Opus'—the Great Work—with four stages marked by color changes.

buildup.text: Nigredo (blackening) was the first stage: destroying the original substance through heating or decomposition.

discovery.text: Albedo (whitening) followed: purifying the remains by washing and distilling until a white substance appeared.

twist.text: Citrinitas (yellowing) was the awakening—a transitional stage many alchemists claimed to reach but couldn't verify.

climax.text: Rubedo (reddening) was the final stage: the creation of the philosopher's stone—a perfect red substance.

punchline.text: Black, white, yellow, red. Four colors on the road to perfection that no one completed.

quiz.question: What are the four stages of the alchemical Great Work?
